Title: Synthetically derived BiAux modulates auxin co-receptor activity to stimulate lateral root formation

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Auxin receptor F-box protein; leucine-rich repeat (LRR) protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates auxin signaling to stimulate prebranch site formation, founder cell specification, and lateral root emergence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for BiAux-induced increase in DR5 expression sites and lateral root-associated auxin responses; BiAux activity is lost/reduced when TIR1 function is combined with afb2 mutation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (tir1 single and tir1 afb2 double/higher-order mutants); DR5::LUC phenotyping; molecular docking; yeast two-hybrid co-receptor assembly assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root tissues; assayed in DR5::LUC seedlings during lateral root formation
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ASK1; IAA1/AXR5; IAA3/SHY2; IAA18/POTENT; IAA28; IAA7/AXR2; IAA14/SLR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TIR1 is a required auxin co-receptor component for BiAux signaling in roots. 2. BiAux binds near, but distinct from, the canonical auxin pocket and enhances TIR1 interaction with selected Aux/IAA proteins in the presence of IAA. 3. TIR1 contributes to BiAux-mediated founder cell specification and lateral root formation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BiAux treatment; IAA co-treatment; root illumination/dark growth assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper studies BiAux modulation of auxin signaling rather than nutrient metabolism; TIR1 is a signaling co-receptor, not a metabolic enzyme.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Auxin receptor F-box protein; leucine-rich repeat (LRR) protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates auxin signaling to promote lateral root developmental outputs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AFB2 is necessary together with TIR1 for BiAux responsiveness; afb2-containing double mutants lose BiAux-induced DR5 expression increase
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; DR5::LUC assays; docking; yeast two-hybrid interaction assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root tissues during lateral root formation assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AFB2 is a principal receptor component mediating BiAux effects. 2. BiAux preferentially enhances AFB2 interaction with IAA18 and IAA28 in the presence of IAA. 3. Genetic data support AFB2 specificity compared with AFB1/AFB3.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a signaling receptor; not a nutrient metabolism gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances auxin co-receptor function linked to founder cell specification and lateral root initiation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BiAux enhances TIR1/AFB2-IAA28 interaction and promotes IAA28 degradation; proper IAA28 degradation is required for full BiAux-induced founder cell formation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid assays; pHS::IAA28-VENUS degradation imaging; mutant analysis in iaa28-1; SKP2Bp::GUS and DR5::LUC phenotyping
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root meristem and lateral root founder cell specification context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAA28 is a direct BiAux-responsive Aux/IAA component in the lateral root pathway. 2. BiAux increases IAA28 interaction with TIR1/AFB2 and tends to accelerate IAA28 degradation. 3. iaa28-1 attenuates BiAux-induced DR5 and founder cell responses, showing functional requirement for IAA28 turnover.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BiAux treatment; IAA co-treatment; heat-shock induction for IAA28-VENUS assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Signaling repressor, not a nutrient metabolism gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates root clock-associated auxin signaling linked to prebranch site formation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BiAux enhances interaction of IAA18/POTENT with TIR1/AFB2/AFB1 in yeast and strongly boosts DR5::LUC signal in potent mutant
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid interaction assays; mutant DR5::LUC phenotyping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAA18/POTENT is a BiAux-sensitive Aux/IAA repressor associated with root clock regulation. 2. BiAux strengthens its interaction with selected auxin receptors, particularly TIR1 and AFB2. 3. Genetic and reporter data connect BiAux action to the IAA18/POTENT-controlled oscillatory module.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not shown to undergo direct degradation by BiAux in this paper; signaling role only.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes auxin signaling outputs leading to prebranch site formation and lateral root emergence
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation downstream of auxin signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Lateral root development gene module; includes LBD29 and other ARF7-dependent BiAux-induced genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates auxin-responsive genes required for prebranch site formation, founder cell specification, and lateral root emergence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential downstream transcriptional effector required for BiAux-induced DR5 site increase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: arf7 abolishes the BiAux-induced increase in DR5 expression sites, whereas arf19 does not; BiAux also tends to increase ARF7 expression in mature root regions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (arf7-1 vs arf19-1); DR5::LUC assays; transcriptomic overlap analyses; pARF7::LUC expression profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mature root region; lateral root development context
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: BiAux-enhanced auxin co-receptor signaling via TIR1 and AFB2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF7, but not ARF19, is required for BiAux-induced increase in DR5 expression sites. 2. BiAux-upregulated genes are enriched for ARF7-dependent lateral root genes. 3. BiAux likely acts through the ARF7 pathway to promote founder cell specification and lateral root emergence.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Developmental signaling regulator, not a nutrient metabolism regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not decisive in this study; used to demonstrate specificity for ARF7
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: arf19 mutant still responds to BiAux similarly to wild type in DR5 site induction, indicating ARF19 is dispensable for BiAux function
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF19 is not required for BiAux-induced DR5 site increase. 2. Its inclusion demonstrates specificity of BiAux dependence on ARF7 rather than general ARF function.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive specificity control rather than primary positive mediator.
